steps.title: Identify the contradiction
steps.content: Research contradicts the myth: the myth says tulip mania was “frenzied” and “disastrous,” but research shows it “was neither as frenzied nor as disastrous as has been thought.”

steps.title: Match the consequence
steps.content:
Therefore works because it expresses consequence: the exaggerated myth deserves skepticism.

The other choices don't establish consequence—”for example” introduces an example, “by contrast” suggests opposition, and “nevertheless” implies persistence despite evidence.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
